name: Warps
description: Meta-modulator that blends and combines two audio signals through seven cross-modulation algorithms: crossfade, wavefolder, diode ring-mod, digital ring-mod, bitwise XOR, comparator/octaver and 20-band vocoder. Includes an internal digital carrier oscillator (sine/tri/saw or saw/pulse/LP-noise) with V/Oct tracking.

controls.name: ALGORITHM Knob
controls.description: Selects/morphs continuously between the seven cross-modulation algorithms. The knob's LED colour reflects the current algorithm.
controls.detail: 1 Crossfade · 2 Wavefolder · 3 Diode ring-mod · 4 Digital ring-mod · 5 Bitwise XOR · 6 Comparator/octaver · 7 Vocoder

controls.name: TIMBRE
controls.description: Algorithm-dependent tone/harmonics control — brightness, fold amount, ring-mod drive, XOR bit mask or vocoder formant shift.
controls.detail: Crossfade: mix · Folder: fold amount · Ring-mods: gain/clip · XOR: which bits · Compare: morph · Vocoder: formant shift

controls.name: LEVEL 1 (Carrier amplitude / Internal osc frequency)
controls.description: When the internal oscillator is OFF, sets the carrier amplitude or the amount of AM from LEVEL 1 CV. When the internal oscillator is ON, sets the internal oscillator frequency.
controls.detail: Gain can be pushed above unity for a warm overdrive

controls.name: LEVEL 2 (Modulator amplitude)
controls.description: Modulator input gain, or depth of AM from LEVEL 2 CV when a cable is present (LEVEL 2 CV is normalled to +5V when unpatched).
controls.detail: Gain can be pushed above unity for a warm overdrive

controls.name: INT. OSC Button
controls.description: Enables the internal digital oscillator (replacing the external carrier) and cycles its waveform. LED colour shows current waveform; LED off = external carrier.
controls.detail: Cross-mod waves: sine, triangle, saw. Vocoder waves: saw, pulse, LP-filtered noise.

controls.name: ALGORITHM CV (sum)
controls.description: Voltage on the ALGORITHM CV input is summed with the knob position — no attenuverter; enables continuous morphing between adjacent algorithms.
controls.detail: No dedicated attenuverter on the panel

inputs.name: LEVEL 1 CV
inputs.description: Carrier amplitude/AM CV when internal oscillator is OFF; acts as V/Oct input for the internal oscillator when it is ON.

inputs.name: LEVEL 2 CV
inputs.description: Modulator amplitude CV. Normalled to +5V when unpatched — when nothing is patched here the modulator runs at the gain set by LEVEL 2.

inputs.name: ALGORITHM CV
inputs.description: CV added to the ALGORITHM knob position for algorithm selection/morph.

inputs.name: TIMBRE CV
inputs.description: CV added to the TIMBRE knob position.

inputs.name: IN 1 (Carrier)
inputs.description: External carrier audio input. Expects modular level (typically 10Vpp, up to 20Vpp). When the internal oscillator is ON this input phase-modulates the oscillator, or feeds external noise into the LP filter for the noise waveform.

inputs.name: IN 2 (Modulator)
inputs.description: External modulator audio input. Expects modular level.

outputs.name: OUT (Modulator output)
outputs.description: Main processed output of the selected cross-modulation algorithm.

outputs.name: AUX
outputs.description: Auxiliary output. When the internal oscillator is OFF, carries the post-VCA sum of carrier and modulator. When the internal oscillator is ON, carries the raw internal oscillator waveform.

behaviors.name: Internal carrier oscillator
behaviors.description: Built-in digital oscillator replaces the external carrier (IN 1). LEVEL 1 becomes its frequency knob, LEVEL 1 CV becomes a V/Oct input, and IN 1 becomes a phase-modulation input (or external noise source for the noise wave). Waveforms are sine/tri/saw for cross-mod algorithms and saw/pulse/LP-noise for the vocoder.

behaviors.name: Algorithm morphing
behaviors.description: Algorithm CV is summed with the knob position and smoothly morphs between adjacent algorithms, so crossfade → folder → ring-mod → XOR → comparator → vocoder is a continuous sweep.

behaviors.name: Vocoder (algorithm 7)
behaviors.description: 20-band analog-style channel vocoder with 20 analysis + 20 synthesis third-octave 48dB filters. IN 1 is the carrier (or the internal osc on saw/pulse/LP-noise), IN 2 is the modulator (speech/drums/noise). TIMBRE warps the formant mapping (shifts formants up/down). Turning ALGORITHM further clockwise increases envelope-follower release; fully CW freezes the modulator spectrum.

behaviors.name: Bitwise XOR (algorithm 5)
behaviors.description: Carrier and modulator are converted to 16-bit integers and XOR'ed bit by bit. TIMBRE selects which bits take part — lo-fi digital grit and chiptune harmonics.

behaviors.name: Comparator / octaver (algorithm 6)
behaviors.description: Bank of comparison operations (e.g. replace the negative half of the carrier with the modulator, output whichever signal has larger absolute value, etc.). TIMBRE morphs between them — some settings resemble an octave-pedal fuzz.

behaviors.name: AUX behaves contextually
behaviors.description: AUX is the post-VCA carrier+modulator sum when the internal oscillator is OFF, but becomes the raw internal-oscillator waveform when the oscillator is ON — useful as an extra VCO output.

behaviors.name: Frequency-shifter easter egg
behaviors.trigger: All cables out, TIMBRE and both LEVELs fully CCW, then dial sequence 2-4-3-6-1-5 on the ALGORITHM knob, pressing the button after each number
behaviors.description: Hidden single-sideband frequency shifter. ALGORITHM sets shift amount (centre = no shift, CW positive, CCW negative; linear to 50Hz then exponential). LEVEL 1 sets feedback, LEVEL 2 sets dry/wet, TIMBRE balances upper/lower sidebands. With INT. OSC OFF it becomes a quadrature cross-modulator that multiplies the analytic signals of IN 1 and IN 2.

behaviors.name: Calibration
behaviors.trigger: Hold INT. OSC for 5 seconds (all CV disconnected)
behaviors.description: Calibrates internal oscillator V/Oct tracking via LEVEL 1 CV using C2 (1V) and C4 (3V), with LEVEL 2 CV left floating (patched cable, other end unplugged). Knob blinks turquoise then fuchsia to guide the steps.

behaviors.name: Normalisation recalibration
behaviors.description: Run this if the sound becomes choppy/morse-like. Patch a constant positive voltage to LEVEL 1 CV, hold INT. OSC 10s until LEDs blink red, remove all cables and press INT. OSC to confirm.

patchIdeas.name: Internal-oscillator ring-mod voice
patchIdeas.patch: INT. OSC ON (sine) · Algorithm 3 (diode RM) or 4 (digital RM) · external VCO into IN 1 (carrier, external) — or use internal osc and patch the melodic VCO into IN 2 as modulator · V/Oct (from sequencer) into LEVEL 1 CV tracks the internal osc · TIMBRE adds drive · OUT = classic ring-mod bells
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 3 (diode RM)
* INT. OSC: ON (sine)

- Melodic VCO (Out) -> Warps (IN 2)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Warps (LEVEL 1 CV)
- Warps (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Modular vocoder
patchIdeas.patch: Algorithm 7 (vocoder, knob fully CW) · chord VCO into IN 1 (carrier) · drum loop or speech into IN 2 (modulator) · TIMBRE shifts formants · OUT = talking chords. Nudging ALGORITHM back from full CW reduces the freeze time.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 7 (vocoder)
* INT. OSC: OFF

- Chord VCO (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- Drum Loop (Out) -> Warps (IN 2)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Chord VCO (V/Oct)
- Warps (OUT) -> Reverb (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Morphing algorithm effect
patchIdeas.patch: Slow LFO into ALGORITHM CV sweeps crossfade → folder → RM → XOR → comparator → vocoder · TIMBRE CV from an envelope · one module delivers evolving processing that rewrites itself rhythmically
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
- VCO 1 (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Warps (IN 2)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Warps (ALGORITHM CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> Warps (TIMBRE CV)
- Warps (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: XOR bit-crushed drums
patchIdeas.patch: Algorithm 5 (bitwise XOR) · drum loop into IN 1 · square LFO or second drum layer into IN 2 · TIMBRE selects which bits XOR (heavy to the right) · OUT into mixer for chiptune-grit drums
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 5 (XOR)

- Drum Loop (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- Square LFO (Out) -> Warps (IN 2)
- Warps (OUT) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Frequency-shifter shimmer (easter egg)
patchIdeas.patch: Unlock the frequency-shifter easter egg (cables out, LEVELs+TIMBRE CCW, dial 2-4-3-6-1-5) · patch pad into IN 1 · ALGORITHM near centre for subtle shift · LEVEL 1 adds feedback · LEVEL 2 dry/wet · OUT + AUX into reverb for shimmer trail
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Mode: frequency shifter (easter egg)

- Pad (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- Warps (OUT) -> Reverb (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Feedback drone
patchIdeas.patch: Algorithm 2 (wavefolder) · single VCO into IN 1 · OUT patched back into IN 2 for audio-rate feedback · TIMBRE dialed to the edge of chaos · OUT through filter/VCA for a self-oscillating warped drone
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 2 (folder)

- VCO (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- Warps (OUT) -> Warps (IN 2) // feedback
- Warps (OUT) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Comparator/octaver bass grit
patchIdeas.patch: Algorithm 6 (comparator) · sub VCO into IN 1 · second VCO detuned into IN 2 · TIMBRE morphs between comparison laws · OUT delivers octave-pedal-style grit · AUX gives the post-VCA mix for a parallel layer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 6 (comparator)

- Sub VCO (Out) -> Warps (IN 1)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> Warps (IN 2)
- Warps (OUT) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
- Warps (AUX) -> Mixer (In) // carrier+modulator mix

patchIdeas.name: Warps as a dual VCO (AUX trick)
patchIdeas.patch: INT. OSC ON · Algorithm 1 (crossfade) · no external inputs · LEVEL 1 sets internal-osc pitch, LEVEL 1 CV is V/Oct · OUT delivers the processed/crossfaded signal while AUX carries the raw internal oscillator — one module, two usable voices
patchIdeas.diagram:
Warps:
* Algorithm: 1 (crossfade)
* INT. OSC: ON (saw)

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Warps (LEVEL 1 CV)
- Warps (AUX) -> Filter A (In) // raw osc
- Warps (OUT) -> Filter B (In) // processed voice
